tasks.title: Document --include-draft for run one/loop in docs/cli.md
evidence: docs/cli.md: `ralph run` section lists examples but does not mention --include-draft.
crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs: run command help examples include `--include-draft` for both `run one` and `run loop`.
crates/ralph/src/agent.rs: RunAgentArgs defines the `--include-draft` flag used by run commands.
plan: Add a short description of --include-draft in the `ralph run` docs, explaining that draft tasks can be selected when enabled.
Add or update examples for `ralph run one` and `ralph run loop` that include --include-draft (matching CLI help ordering).
Verify docs wording matches the CLI help text in crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs and the flag definition in crates/ralph/src/agent.rs.
notes: The --include-draft flag is defined on RunAgentArgs in crates/ralph/src/agent.rs and surfaced via run.rs help examples.

tasks.title: Clarify --force behavior for dirty-repo bypass in run/scan
evidence: docs/cli.md: Global `--force` flag description only calls out bypassing stale queue locks (no mention of clean-repo bypass for run/scan).
docs/cli.md: `ralph run` section notes clean-repo checks and `--force` bypass, but there is no dedicated `ralph scan` section describing clean-repo behavior.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs: run_one_impl calls gitutil::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(..., force, gitutil::RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS).
crates/ralph/src/scan_cmd.rs: run_scan calls gitutil::require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths(..., opts.force, [".ralph/queue.json", ".ralph/done.json"]).
crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs: after_long_help already documents the clean-repo check and `--force` bypass.
crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs: after_long_help has no clean-repo or `--force` note.
plan: Update docs/cli.md global flag description to mention clean-repo bypass for run/scan, and add a short `ralph scan` section that explains the clean-repo check plus `--force` behavior with examples.
Add a clean-repo/`--force` note to `ralph scan` CLI help text in crates/ralph/src/cli/scan.rs; confirm run help remains accurate.
Verify help/doc alignment by checking `ralph run --help` and `ralph scan --help` output (or equivalent in docs) before closing.
notes: Run docs/help already mention the clean-repo bypass; the gap is global flag wording and scan documentation/help text.

tasks.title: Refactor TUI core module to reduce responsibilities
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: 2879 lines (wc -l), still a single file holding state models, queue IO, palette/actions, and runtime execution wiring.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: in-file tests (`#[cfg(test)]` at line 2271).
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs header enumerates multiple features (queue browsing, editing, execution, loop, archive, palette), indicating multiple responsibilities in one module.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs re-exports events/render symbols (`pub use events::...`, `pub use render::draw_ui`), so mod.rs is the central TUI entry point.
plan: Map cohesive sections inside tui/mod.rs (state/models, filters, queue IO, palette/actions, execution/loop) and define the target module boundaries.

Extract sections into new submodules under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (for example: state.rs, filters.rs, actions.rs, persistence.rs, palette.rs, execution.rs) and keep mod.rs as a thin re-export/bootstrapping facade.
Move the in-file tests into a dedicated module (for example, crates/ralph/src/tui/tests.rs or crates/ralph/src/tui/tests/mod.rs), update module declarations, and run `cargo test -p ralph tui`.
notes: The tui directory currently only contains mod.rs, events.rs, and render.rs, so new core submodules will be added alongside those existing files.

tasks.title: Split TUI event handling into focused modules
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/events.rs: 1853 lines (wc -l) with all key-event dispatch and per-mode handlers in one module.
crates/ralph/src/tui/events.rs: #[cfg(test)] test module starts at line 1123.
crates/ralph/src/tui/: directory currently contains events.rs, mod.rs, and render.rs (no per-mode event submodules yet).
plan: Enumerate AppMode variants and the current handler functions in events.rs, then define a per-mode module layout plus a minimal dispatcher surface.

Create a crates/ralph/src/tui/events/ module tree (for example, normal.rs, help.rs, editing.rs, create.rs, run.rs) and move mode handlers into those files, wiring them through a small dispatcher in events.rs or events/mod.rs and updating mod.rs exports.
Move the events.rs tests into a dedicated test module under crates/ralph/src/tui/events/ (tests.rs or mod.rs) and run `cargo test -p ralph tui`.
notes: events.rs already documents per-mode dispatch, so the refactor should preserve that flow while reducing file size.

tasks.title: Split TUI rendering into component modules
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs: 1721 lines (wc -l) containing all render logic in a single file.
crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs: in-file tests remain (`#[cfg(test)]` at line 1614).
crates/ralph/src/tui/: directory still only has mod.rs, events.rs, and render.rs (no render submodules).
plan: Map the render.rs sections (task list, details, logs, overlays, status/help chrome) into component boundaries and pick a module layout under crates/ralph/src/tui/render/.
Create the render/ module tree and move component render helpers into those files while keeping a thin draw_ui facade (render.rs or render/mod.rs) that assembles the UI.
Move the in-file tests into a dedicated render test module, update mod.rs exports, and run `cargo test -p ralph tui`.
notes: tui/ currently has only mod.rs, events.rs, and render.rs, so the refactor will add a new render/ module directory and adjust exports.

tasks.title: Split TUI contract tests into scenario-focused files
evidence: crates/ralph/tests/tui_contract_test.rs: 981 lines (wc -l), still a single integration test file covering multiple TUI behaviors.
crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs: shared test helpers already exist and can host common TUI setup when splitting scenarios.
plan: Identify TUI contract scenarios in tui_contract_test.rs and group them into feature-focused test files (for example, search, edit, archive, loop, queue navigation).
Move shared fixtures/setup into crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs (or a new tui-specific helper module) and update the split tests to reuse that helper.
Ensure the new test files are picked up by cargo test and run the TUI contract tests to confirm behavior remains unchanged.
notes: Test support helpers already live in crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs, so reuse that module unless TUI-specific helpers need a separate file.

tasks.title: Split TUI rendering tests into smaller files
evidence: crates/ralph/tests/tui_rendering_test.rs: 887 lines (wc -l) containing 53 rendering tests in a single file.
crates/ralph/tests/tui_rendering_test.rs: shared helpers (make_test_task, make_test_queue, setup_test_terminal, get_rendered_output, output_contains) are defined inline alongside the tests.
rg "TestBackend" crates/ralph/tests shows only tui_rendering_test.rs uses the TestBackend harness, so there is no shared TUI rendering helper module yet.
plan: Group the existing rendering tests by area (task list/details, modes/dialogs, execution/logs, help/overlay/footer, layout/scrolling) and decide to target test file names under crates/ralph/tests/.
Extract the shared rendering harness into a helper module (either a new tui_rendering_support.rs or an extension of test_support.rs) and update all split tests to import it.
Move the grouped tests into the new files, remove the old monolithic file, and run `cargo test -p ralph tui_rendering` to confirm coverage stays intact.
notes: tui_rendering_test.rs now mixes 50+ scenarios with the harness helpers in the same file, so a dedicated rendering support module will reduce duplication when splitting.

tasks.title: Refactor git_repo_integration_test.rs into smaller scenario tests
evidence: crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_integration_test.rs: 767 lines (wc -l), still a single integration test file.
crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_integration_test.rs: 10 #[test] cases (rg --count "#[test]") bundled together.

crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_integration_test.rs: helper functions (ralph_bin, run_in_dir, git_init, write_valid_single_todo_queue, configure_runner, configure_ci_gate, create_fake_runner, create_executable_script) are defined inline alongside tests.
crates/ralph/tests/git_repo_integration_test.rs: imports mod test_support, indicating shared helpers already exist for tests.
plan: Catalog the git repo integration scenarios and map them to new test files under crates/ralph/tests/ (e.g., init_validate, runner_config, ci_gate, dirty_repo).
Extract shared repo setup/helpers into a common module (extend crates/ralph/tests/test_support.rs or add a git_repo-specific support module) and update tests to use it.
Move the scenario tests into the new files, delete the monolithic git_repo_integration_test.rs, and run `cargo test -p ralph git_repo`.
notes: The current file already defines multiple helper functions inline and uses mod test_support, so helper extraction can build on that existing shared module.

tasks.title: Make reordering tasks in queue easier by adding functionality to TUI
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/events.rs: Normal-mode key handling only moves selection (Up/Down or j/k) and provides no action to reorder tasks in queue order.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: move_up/move_down only adjust selection/scroll and never mutate queue.tasks, so TUI navigation does not change task order.
crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: palette_entries lists run/archive/edit/filter/status/priority actions, but no reorder command is exposed.
crates/ralph/src/tui/render.rs: help overlay lists navigation/actions/quick changes but does not mention any task reordering shortcut.
plan: Add App methods to move task up/down in queue order (swap in queue.tasks), update filtered indices/selection, set dirty flags, and surface a status message when at boundaries.
Wire new reorder actions into TUI input (events.rs normal mode + palette command wiring in mod.rs) and document keybindings in the help overlay (render.rs).
Add tests covering reorder success and boundary/no-op behavior in the TUI event tests, update help overlay assertions, and run `cargo test -p ralph tui`.
notes: Selection movement already exists via move_up/move_down; reordering will need to operate on queue.tasks while respecting filtered_indices so the visible selection stays coherent.

tasks.title: Allow rejected dependencies to unblock runnable tasks (or update docs/tests to match)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: StatusArg::Rejected help text says dependents can proceed, implying rejected should unblock dependents.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs: are_dependencies_met requires each dependency to have status == TaskStatus::Done (Rejected is treated as unmet).
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/tests.rs: test_next_runnable_task_skips_rejected_dep asserts a rejected dependency blocks runnable selection.
plan: Confirm the intended dependency policy for rejected tasks (unblock dependents vs. keep blocked) by reviewing CLI help expectations and any project guidance.

Align queue dependency logic and comments with the chosen policy (update are_dependencies_met and related error messaging if rejected should satisfy dependencies, or update StatusArg::Rejected help text if it should not).
Adjust/add queue operation tests to cover the chosen behavior and ensure rejected dependency cases remain explicitly verified.
notes: Current code treats rejected dependencies as unmet, which conflicts with the CLI status help text that claims dependents can proceed.

custom_fields.stale_path: queue CLI moved to crates/ralph/src/cli/queue/ (mod.rs); update scope/evidence references
